From turning messy lecture notes into podcasts to testing your knowledge with custom quizzes, we rounded up six easy ways you can use Gemini to help you prep finals ⬇️ 

Learn more and get started today: https://goo.gle/4ts9PIH

We’re introducing Deep Research and Deep Research Max via the Gemini API — our new autonomous research agents powered by Gemini 3.1 Pro.

Think of these “autonomous agents” as your ultimate research partner for comprehensive reports. With your guidance, they go down the rabbit hole for you: searching the web and your custom data, generating visuals, and connecting dots to build professional-grade research reports — all from a single API call.

Meet the two new agents:

⚡ Deep Research: Built for speed. We’ve significantly reduced latency and cost, making it ideal for real-time applications where fast, high-quality answers are critical. 

🧠 Deep Research Max: Built for depth. Leverages extended “think” time to reason, search and refine. Kick off complex research requests overnight, and wake up to fully cited, comprehensive research reports by morning.

With just one prompt, these agents can:

🌐 Blend the open web with your private files and custom data connectors via MCP to deliver fully cited, fact-checked reports you can see.

📊 Natively generate charts & infographics to automatically visualize complex data directly in your document.

📋 Collaborate with you: You can review, guide and refine the agent's research plan before it begins, keeping you in the driver's seat.

Deep Research and Deep Research Max allow you to tap into the same engine powering the Gemini app, Google Search and NotebookLM. Both agents are live today in public preview via the Gemini API (coming soon to Google Cloud).

Dive deeper into developer features like arbitrary MCP and real-time streaming, and see how to get started → https://goo.gle/4vH3k6b

Google AI Studio is now included in Google AI Pro and Ultra plans at no extra cost. ✨ 

Subscribers now get increased usage limits and access to our Nano Banana Pro and Gemini Pro models — giving you more runway to turn your ideas into working apps in minutes with predictable costs.

Plus, your Google AI plan now serves as a “low-setup” billing bridge, giving builders an easy entry point to prototype, then scale with API keys when you’re ready — all right in AI Studio.

These benefits are rolling out now to all AI Pro and Ultra subscribers, just sign in to AI Studio with your subscriber account to access your benefits.

Learn more → https://goo.gle/48efw4t

We’ve partnered with Vodafone to scale AI tools that help customers across Europe connect and communicate. Watch Rick Osterloh, SVP Platforms & Devices chat with Vodafone Group CEO Margherita Della Valle about how they’re applying Google AI at scale to drive productivity, simplify operations and deliver better customer experiences.

Today, we’re introducing a smarter way to plan your PTO. With individual hotel price tracking in Google Search, you can now monitor your favorite spots and get email notifications when prices change — just like you can with flights. Here’s how it works: 

1️⃣ Head to Google Search and look up a hotel by name and add your chosen dates.

2️⃣ Tap the new price tracking toggle on.

3️⃣ Voila! You’ll get email alerts if rates change significantly during your dates. 

You can also track individual hotel prices at google.com/hotels. Just select a hotel listing and look for the price tracking toggle on the Overview or Prices tab. 

Learn more travel hacks from Google → https://goo.gle/4tdvkg9
👋 Calling all students: We’re sharing six easy ways you can use Gemini to help you during exam season. Because finals week shouldn’t have to be the most stressful week of your year.

We’re introducing a new Search experience in Google Chrome that lets you open webpages side-by-side with AI Mode — no tab switching required. 

Now, you’ll be able to compare details and ask follow-up questions while still maintaining the context of your search.

These updates to AI Mode in Chrome are now available in the U.S., and we’ll expand soon to more places around the world.  Learn more → https://goo.gle/4cO9oCr

We’re introducing new ways for Personal Intelligence and our best image generation model, Nano Banana 2, to generate images that reflect your life, interests, and the things you care about most.

Personal Intelligence securely connects the dots across Google apps like Google Photos and Gmail so Gemini can inherently understand your likes and interests. This allows you to skip long, complex prompts, so a simple request like "design my dream house" now automatically can reflect your unique style and taste. 🏡

Gemini can even use your labeled Google Photos library of you and your loved ones as a visual guide for your creations.

Try asking Gemini things like: “Create a claymation image of me and my family enjoying our favorite activity,” and watch your inner circle come to life, automatically.

You’re always in control: This remains an opt-in experience that you can adjust in your settings at any time.

It’s rolling out now for Google AI Plus, Pro, and Ultra subscribers in the U.S., and we plan to bring this to Gemini in Chrome desktops and more users soon.

Learn more → goo.gle/4cSDCDp

We're live streaming the #GoogleCloudNext Opening Keynote! 

Join Thomas Kurian and industry experts on April 22, where they'll introduce the blueprint for the agentic enterprise.

Select the 'attend' button to get notified when we're live. And register for a complimentary digital ticket to access select sessions on demand → https://goo.gle/4bWhVS6

See you in Vegas (and here on LinkedIn)!

Tammy, a recruiter for Google Cloud AI, shares tips for acing our AI/ML-focused technical interviews.

Ready to explore our open roles? Apply today → https://goo.gle/4rx4sWX

In 2025, we took a major step forward in keeping our ad platforms safe, thanks to Gemini.

By analyzing hundreds of billions of signals, our AI models now better understand intent, helping us spot and block malicious content with incredible precision.

A few highlights from our 2025 Ads Safety Report:
🛡️ Gemini caught over 99% of policy-violating ads before they were ever served
⛔ Our teams blocked or removed over 8.3 billion bad ads
📉 And we reduced incorrect advertiser suspensions by 80%

See how we're using AI to stay ahead of bad actors and keep people safe online → https://goo.gle/4coNUMx

At our inaugural AI for the Economy Forum with MIT FutureTech, we brought together economists, industry leaders, policymakers and workforce training experts to discuss AI’s impact on jobs and the economy. We believe that by working together we can navigate the AI transition successfully. We also shared new investments focused on better understanding how AI will change the economy, training and apprenticeships:

-A new AI & Economy Research Program to answer pressing questions about AI's impact
-New AI training for rural healthcare workers through a $10 million Google.org initiative in collaboration with the Johnson & Johnson Foundation 
-$10 million in Google.org funding to support the Manufacturing Institute in its efforts to train U.S. workers in critical AI skills, and new apprenticeships for manufacturing employees as a pathway to new jobs
-Expanded Google.org support for researchers exploring public policy solutions

Learn more about how we're working across sectors to get our economy AI-ready from James Manyika ⬇️

Family Link is our app that parents can use to manage accounts and set ground rules for their children online. Mindy Brooks, VP Product Management, shares three tips to help families navigate the digital world safely and confidently. 

See all of our tools and resources on Family Link to help you set the right boundaries for your family → https://lnkd.in/e4gq4wuP

The Gemini app is now available natively on Mac, bringing you a faster, more integrated way to get AI help right on your desktop. 🍎

With a quick keyboard shortcut (Option + Space), you can bring up Gemini from anywhere on your screen, getting you the answers you need without switching tabs.

Here’s a few ways you can use it to streamline your work:

🖼️ Share your window: Get answers based on the documents, code, data or anything else you're looking at.

🎨 Stay in your creative flow: Quickly generate images with Nano Banana or videos with Veo without leaving your workspace.

💻 Quick access: Whether you’re drafting a report or building a complex spreadsheet, help is now just a keyboard shortcut away.

Available globally for users on macOS 15 and up at no cost. Download the Gemini app for Mac today → https://gemini.google/mac/

Sundar Pichai and Neal Mohan were named to the TIME100 Most Influential People of 2026 list. Sundar was highlighted for his leadership in the AI era, emphasizing Google’s startup-like nimbleness in launching innovative AI products — like Nano Banana, AI Studio, NotebookLM, Gemini CLI and Antigravity. Neal was recognized as an innovator in digital media for leading YouTube to be the most popular video provider on U.S. televisions.

Check out the full list: https://lnkd.in/gExHy3de

Today, we’re introducing Skills in Google Chrome, so you can build one-click workflows for your most frequently used AI prompts — like scanning long documents for important information or  generating side-by-side comparisons across multiple tabs.

When you write a prompt that you’ll want to use again, you can save it as a Skill directly from your chat history. The next time you need it, select your saved Skill in Gemini in Chrome by typing forward slash ( / ) or clicking the plus sign ( + ) button, and your Skill will run on the page you’re viewing, along with any other tabs you select.  

We’re also launching a library of ready-to-use Skills for common tasks and workflows. You’ll be able to add them as a saved Skills, and can customize them to better fit your needs by updating the prompt. 

Learn more → https://goo.gle/4cbNskD

Curious about quantum? Step onto Google’s Quantum AI Campus to get answers to some of the world’s top trending quantum questions. For World Quantum Day, experts Jenna and Andrew break down the basics of quantum mechanics, the purpose behind quantum computers, as well as set the record straight on how this technology could actually be used.

After 12 years as a game designer in South Korea, Hyunuk decided to hit reset on his career. Going back to study Computer Science allowed him to pivot into his current role as a Developer Advocate on the Cloud Advocacy team. “My specific journey with Google started with a summer internship at the New York office,” he explains. “That experience led me to return as a full-time Developer Advocate, initially starting at the Seattle South Lake Union office. Then, exactly a year later, I transferred to the beautiful Kirkland office, where I am today!”

Hyunuk spends his days making complex data and AI tools more approachable. By applying the same principles he used in gaming, he creates technical content, videos and step-by-step guides for products, and helps developers unlock the full potential of our tech so they feel empowered to build. “By weaving in gamification elements from my previous career, I aim to build highly engaging, interactive experiences that inspire developers,” he says.

His advice for those eyeing a pivot? "Career paths aren't linear sprints; they are marathons without a defined end. Don't compare your "behind-the-scenes" struggles with everyone else's "highlight reel" on social media, he says. “Your timeline is yours alone. It’s never too late to reset and redefine your goals.”

Want to join the Google Kirkland office? Explore our open roles → https://goo.gle/4mBD7Sr

Our offices in Mountain View are home to a host of wildlife, thanks in part to our ongoing conservation efforts: monarch butterflies, honeybees, rare birds and more. But in January, the Google campus got one of its most unlikely visitors yet: a six-month-old California sea lion. Later affectionately named Babymac, the wayward pup had somehow wandered far from home and wound up in one of our parking lots.

Babymac was quickly rescued with the help of concerned Googlers, our Global Security Operations Center (GSOC) team and the Mountain View police, who kept him safe until responders from The Marine Mammal Center were able to transport him to the center for rehabilitation. He faced a long road to recovery from severe malnutrition and other maladies.

Fortunately, Babymac thrived in his treatment at The Marine Mammal Center. After graduating from a special fish smoothie mixture to eating whole herrings on his own, Babymac successfully gained the weight he needed to. In March, he passed a release exam, and was safely returned to the wild in Point Reyes, California.

“We are thrilled Babymac made such positive strides in recovery and was able to be released back to his ocean home,” says Dr. Emily Whitmer, a clinical veterinarian at The Marine Mammal Center.

As for future visits? “We always enjoy visitors at our offices at Google,” says Robert Ortiz Carlos of GSOC operational support. “But if Babymac decides to visit in the future, he’ll need to be signed in at the kiosk and escorted. Hopefully one trip to the Googleplex is enough for a lifetime.”
